metadata.description: CyberOS - author, audit, and ship tasks under a governed workflow, in any repo.

plugins.name: cyberos
plugins.description: Turn an idea, PRD, or spec into audited tasks (/create-tasks), then ship them (/ship-tasks) through implement -> review -> test -> done. /install installs the machine into any repo; /version checks for updates. HITL required at the two human-acceptance gates; agents never push, merge, deploy, or self-accept.

suggested_prompts.label: Turn this PRD into audited tasks
suggested_prompts.prompt: Use /create-tasks to draft tasks from my PRD, audit them against the rubric, and land them ready_to_implement in docs/tasks/BACKLOG.md. Halt at the PLAN approval gate so I can review the FR set before anything is written.

suggested_prompts.label: Ship the next task
suggested_prompts.prompt: Use /ship-tasks to drive the next eligible FR in docs/tasks/BACKLOG.md through implement -> review -> test. HITL is required: halt at review acceptance and at final acceptance for my verdict, and never set done yourself.

suggested_prompts.label: Install CyberOS in this repo
suggested_prompts.prompt: Run /install to install CyberOS into this repo: autodetect the build/lint/test gates, scaffold the task backlog, and wire the agent entry files. Do not clobber anything that already exists.

suggested_prompts.label: What is ready to implement right now?
suggested_prompts.prompt: Read docs/tasks/BACKLOG.md and tell me which tasks are ready_to_implement, which are blocked and why, and which one /ship-tasks would pick up next.
